name: NetworKit (C++ Graph Analytics for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Technical University of Munich

performance.notes: C++ core with OpenMP parallelism; handles billion-edge graphs; parallel algorithms

agent_readiness.notes: C++ graph analytics with OpenMP parallel algorithms for billion-scale graphs. import networkit as nk. g = nk.Graph(10, weighted=True). g.addEdge(0, 1, 2.0). cc = nk.components.ConnectedComponents(g). cc.run(). nk.centrality.Betweenness(g).run().scores(). Community detection: nk.community.PLM(g) (Louvain variant). Generators: nk.generators.ErdosRenyiGenerator. Streaming graph support. NetworKit designed for web-scale networks (social, web crawl). CLI interface. Integration with pandas/numpy/matplotlib.

gotchas: Non-intuitive API — algorithm objects require explicit .run() call
Binary wheel sizes are large (C++ + OpenMP)
Less Pythonic than NetworkX or igraph
best_when: Billion-scale graph analysis where igraph/NetworkX run out of RAM
Parallel graph algorithms exploiting multi-core CPUs
avoid_when: Small/medium graphs where NetworkX ergonomics suffice
Visualization needs (use igraph or pyvis for plotting)
Teams unfamiliar with explicit algorithm object pattern
summary: NetworKit is a C++ graph analytics library with OpenMP parallelism — designed for billion-edge graphs. Algorithm objects: nk.centrality.Betweenness(g).run().scores(). Community detection: nk.community.PLM(g). Streaming graph support. Handles web-scale social networks where NetworkX and igraph run out of memory. Non-intuitive explicit .run() pattern. Best for scale; use NetworkX or igraph for ergonomics.
